100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On May 7, 1926, the British Empire celebrated a historic milestone as King George V's Silver Jubilee marked 25 years on the throne, with grand festivities planned across the nation. In the United States, the aviation industry buzzed as Charles Lindbergh prepared for his historic transatlantic flight, capturing the nation’s imagination. Meanwhile, in Germany, political tensions rose as the government struggled with economic instability and growing unrest.
